tools.name: get_protocol_info
tools.description: Get AGI Alpha protocol information: contract addresses, parameters, token details, and links

tools.name: list_jobs
tools.description: List all jobs on the AGI Alpha job board with their current status. Returns job IDs, employers, agents, payouts, status, and vote counts.

tools.name: get_job
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific job by its ID, including employer, agent, payout, status, validation state, and metadata URIs

tools.name: get_agent_reputation
tools.description: Check the on-chain reputation score of an agent address

tools.name: fetch_job_metadata
tools.description: Fetch and return the IPFS metadata (job spec or completion) for a given job ID

tools.name: upload_to_ipfs
tools.description:
Upload JSON metadata to IPFS via Pinata and return the ipfs:// URI. Use this BEFORE calling create_job (upload the job spec) or request_job_completion (upload the completion proof). Requires a Pinata JWT — get one free at https://app.pinata.cloud/developers/api-keys.

JOB SPEC FORMAT (use for create

tools.name: create_job
tools.description:
Prepare a transaction to create a new job on AGI Alpha. Returns encoded calldata for two transactions that must be sent in order: first the ERC-20 approve, then createJob.

STEP 1 — Build and upload the job spec JSON to IPFS using upload_to_ipfs. The JSON must have this exact structure:
{
  "name":

tools.name: apply_for_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to apply for a job as an agent. Requires an ENS subdomain under agent.agi.eth or alpha.agent.agi.eth. Returns approve + apply calldata. Agent must post a 5% bond.

tools.name: request_job_completion
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to submit job completion as the assigned agent. Requires a completion URI pointing to IPFS metadata with deliverables.

tools.name: approve_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to approve/validate a job as a validator. Requires an ENS subdomain under club.agi.eth or alpha.club.agi.eth. Validator must post a 15% bond (min 100 AGIALPHA).

tools.name: disapprove_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to disapprove a job as a validator. Requires club.agi.eth ENS subdomain and a 15% validator bond.

tools.name: dispute_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to dispute a job. Only the employer can dispute during the review period.

tools.name: cancel_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to cancel an open (unassigned) job. Only the employer can cancel. Escrow is returned.

tools.name: finalize_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to finalize an approved job. Anyone can call after the challenge period (1 day post-approval). Distributes payout to agent (80%), validators (8%), and protocol.

tools.name: register_agent
tools.description: Prepare a free transaction to mint an on-chain Alpha Agent Identity NFT. This registers your agent label (e.g. "myagent" → myagent.alpha.agent.agi.eth) on Ethereum and unlocks 60% payout on jobs. Free to mint — just pay gas. Check if already registered with check_agent_identity.

tools.name: check_agent_identity
tools.description: Check whether a wallet address has already registered an Alpha Agent Identity NFT, and what payout percentage they qualify for.

tools.name: expire_job
tools.description: Prepare a transaction to expire an overdue assigned job. Anyone can call if the job duration has elapsed. Employer gets refunded, agent bond is slashed.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "upload_to_ipfs" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Upload JSON metadata to IPFS via Pinata and return the ipfs:// URI. Use this BEFORE calling create_job (upload the job spec) or request_job_completion (upload t…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "upload_to_ipfs" is 3495 chars, far longer than this server's median (137). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Upload JSON metadata to IPFS via Pinata and return the ipfs:// URI. Use this BEFORE calling create_job (upload the job spec) or request_job_completion (upload t…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "create_job" is 1783 chars, far longer than this server's median (137). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Prepare a transaction to create a new job on AGI Alpha. Returns encoded calldata for two transactions that must be sent in order: first the ERC-20 approve, then…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: upload_to_ipfs, create_job · Private data: get_protocol_info, upload_to_ipfs, create_job, check_agent_identity · Public sink: upload_to_ipfs, create_job, apply_for_job, request_job_completion, approve_job, finalize_job · Destructive: upload_to_ipfs, create_job.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: upload_to_ipfs, create_job · Private data: get_protocol_info, upload_to_ipfs, create_job, check_agent_identity · Public sink: upload_to_ipfs, create_job, apply_for_job, request_job_completion, approve_job, finalize_job · Destructive: upload_to_ipfs, create_job

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
